title: Customer Support Operations — Current Architecture (Q1 2026)
body:
## Overview

The Customer Support function handles ~6,000 emails per week (~1,200/day) across three regional pods: Americas (24 agents), EMEA (16 agents), APAC (10 agents). 50 agents total. Average email-drafting time is 6 minutes; full handle time including review/research is 9 minutes per ticket. First-response SLA is 4 hours, resolution SLA is 24 hours. Current attainment: 85% first-response, 78% resolution.

## Team

- 50 support agents across 3 regional pods
- Average tenure 1.8 years; attrition 22% annual
- Tier-1 / Tier-2 / Tier-3 specialization
- ~40% of inbound volume is tier-1 (account access, simple billing, status updates)
- ~60% is tier-2 / tier-3 (technical troubleshooting, integration questions, enterprise account escalations)

## Systems

- Intake: AWS SES → Salesforce Service Cloud (case object) for ticket creation
- Knowledge base: Confluence (this space) + Zendesk Guide
- CRM: Salesforce; some legacy cases still in Zendesk during a 2024 migration
- Customer history: read from Salesforce + a Snowflake warehouse with 24h freshness
- Telephony: not in scope — voice support handled separately by call-center team

## Data

- 18 months of historical email threads tagged by category and resolution (~480k tickets)
- Threads stored in Salesforce; export to S3 nightly
- ~70% of current responses use one of 24 template macros — already partially templatized
- Sentiment + CSAT scores attached to ~60% of resolved tickets (post-resolution survey)

## Known pain points

1. Drafting time is the single largest component of handle time — 6 of 9 minutes per ticket on average
2. New agents take 6-8 weeks to ramp on tone and macro selection
3. EMEA pod handles 30% of volume but 45% of complex technical escalations; backlog routinely 12-18 hours during ramp
4. No ML / data science function inside CX. Closest team is the org-level Data Science group, reports to Engineering, currently fully booked through Q3

## Existing automation

- 24 template macros for the most common ticket categories (~70% coverage)
- Salesforce intent classification (rule-based) for routing to Tier-1 vs Tier-2 queue
- Automated CSAT survey 48h after resolution

## Stakeholders

- Executive sponsor: Priya Sharma, SVP Customer Experience
- Operations lead: Daniel Ortiz, VP Customer Experience
- Platform owner: Support Engineering team (8 engineers, mostly Salesforce-focused)
- Compliance partner: Aisha Khan, Privacy & Compliance
